_comment: Cache headers for the heavy static assets — videos, ad creatives, posters, content tier JSONs. These are content-immutable; without long-lived cache headers Vercel re-validates them on every visit, which compounds first-load latency on repeat visits and ad-mode switches. The build output (JS/CSS chunks) already gets long-lived caching via Vite's content-hashed filenames, so we don't need a rule for /assets/index-*.js etc. — Vercel's defaults handle hashed assets correctly.
